text: The patient is positioned supine with slight reverse Trendelenburg. Four 8mm robotic trocars and two double-sided assistant ports are utilized. The robotic surgeon operates a monopolar hook and two bipolar forceps, while the assistant controls a laparoscopic ultrasonic dissector. The round ligament is divided, and the falciform and anterior coronary ligaments are opened. The liver is pushed down using the second bipolar forceps from arm number four, freeing the anterior face of the right hepatic vein.

The surgeon dissects the gallbladder, sections the cystic artery and duct between two hemolog clips, and pushes the cystic stump to the left. This maneuver exposes the right hepatic artery, which is controlled and clipped, and also reveals the portal vein. The left portal vein is identified, and the right portal vein is controlled and secured with tape.

The surgeon uses wristed robotic instruments to place hemoclips on the right portal vein, which is then sectioned. The posterior face of the caudate lobe is exposed, allowing for the release of the inferior vena cava. Accessory hepatic veins are controlled and sectioned between clips.

The surgeon marks the demarcation line on the liver using a monopolar hook, confirmed with an intravenous injection of indocyanine green. The assistant performs parenchymal transection with a CUSA and irrigation device. The robotic surgeon manipulates two bipolar forceps and a monopolar hook, with irrigation enhancing the bipolar efficiency. The segment V hepatic vein is controlled and sectioned at the right edge of the middle hepatic vein. The right hepatic duct is sectioned and ligated with clips after parenchymal transection, exposing the entire face of the inferior vena cava.

The surgeon controls the Segment VIII hepatic vein and sections it at the right edge of the middle hepatic vein. The anterior face of the inferior vena cava is fully exposed. The voluminous right accessory vein is controlled and sectioned using a vascular stapler. The right hepatic vein is also controlled and sectioned with a vascular stapler. The camera is repositioned to arm number three for an improved view of the right hepatic vein.

The Makushi ligament is sectioned, and the right hepatic coronary ligament is divided, completing the mobilization of the liver. The resected specimen is placed in a bag for extraction through a suprapubic incision. The final operative field is shown.
